content.text: Please create PDF file showing the rendering of Mandelbrot set

output.code:
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Generate Mandelbrot set image
def mandelbrot(c, max_iter):
    z = c
    for n in range(max_iter):
        if abs(z) > 2:
            return n
        z = z*z + c
    return max_iter

# Image size and limits
width, height = 800, 800
x_min, x_max = -2.0, 1.0
y_min, y_max = -1.5, 1.5
max_iter = 100

# Generate image
image = np.zeros((height, width))
for i in range(height):
    for j in range(width):
        x = np.linspace(x_min, x_max, width)[j]
        y = np.linspace(y_min, y_max, height)[i]
        c = complex(x, y)
        color = mandelbrot(c, max_iter)
        image[i, j] = color

# Plot and save as PDF
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 8))
plt.imshow(image, extent=(x_min, x_max, y_min, y_max), cmap='twilight')
plt.colorbar()
plt.title('Mandelbrot Set')
plt.xlabel('Re')
plt.ylabel('Im')
plt.savefig('/mnt/data/mandelbrot_set.pdf', format='pdf')
plt.close()

'/mnt/data/mandelbrot_set.pdf'

content.text:
I have created a PDF file illustrating the rendering of the Mandelbrot set. You can download it using the link below:
